title: **Feature Request: Per-node forwarding denylist for repeaters**

messages.author: JdS
messages.text: Feature Request: Per-node forwarding denylist for repeaters We'd like to propose a small but impactful feature: the ability for a repeater operator to block the forwarding of packets from a limited set of specific nodes (identified by public key). The problem In some communities we are experiencing an issue where one or more individuals — possibly unaware of the impact of their behavior — are sending messages at an unusually high frequency, with little to no meaningful content. This creates unnecessary congestion on the network. While this is already annoying under normal conditions, the real concern is resilience: if multiple users behave this way simultaneously, the network could collapse precisely when it matters most — during an emergency. As a repeater operator, I own my hardware and I have a legitimate interest in deciding what my node retransmits. Currently, MeshCore gives me no way to do this selectively — my only option is to disable forwarding entirely, which hurts everyone. The proposed solution A forwarding denylist: a short list of public keys whose packets the repeater will receive but not retransmit. To prevent abuse of this mechanism, we suggest capping the denylist at a small number of entries — perhaps 2 to 5 maximum. This is not meant to be a censorship tool; it's a minimal safety valve to protect network integrity. Why this matters MeshCore is increasingly being used for community resilience and emergency communication. A network that can be degraded or collapsed by a single misbehaving node is a fragile one. Giving responsible repeater operators a limited, auditable way to protect their segment of the network makes the whole ecosystem more robust. We believe this is a reasonable, technically feasible feature that respects the open nature of the protocol while giving operators a basic level of control over their own infrastructure.

messages.reactions_html: 3
